criteria.label: Use chain, islice and batching to process streams without building big lists.
criteria.example:
```python
from itertools import chain, islice
all_docs = chain(local_docs, web_docs)         # no list copy
first_100 = list(islice(all_docs, 100))         # take 100 from the stream
```

criteria.label: Apply lru_cache correctly (pure functions, sensible maxsize).
Use partial to pre-bind arguments and reduce for genuine folds.
criteria.example:
```python
from functools import partial, reduce
log_warn = partial(logger.log, logging.WARNING)
log_warn('cache miss')      # equivalent to logger.log(WARN, 'cache miss')

merged = reduce(lambda d, x: {**d, **x}, list_of_dicts, {})   # actual fold
```

criteria.label: Recognize and avoid one-pass exhaustion and infinite-iterator pitfalls.
criteria.example:
```python
it = iter(stream)
first = list(islice(it, 100))   # consumes first 100
rest  = list(it)                # iterator continues from 101 — fine
list(it)                        # now empty — iterator was already drained
```
